resources.string: Without the two-minute set-up the app hasn\'t learned how your bass sounds, and scores can be unreliable.\n\nYou can always run it later from Settings → Full setup.
Your coach for playing in tune — short games, real scores, and feedback that actually helps.\n\nA few quick questions to set things up for you.
How do you name your notes?
Whatever your teacher uses — every note in the app is written this way.
How much thinking time do you want?
Time to read the note and place your hand before the timer runs out. Scoring is equally fair at every pace.
How long should one round be?
You can always change this in Settings.
How should the app talk to you?
Both say the same thing — one in words, one in numbers.
\"+12.3 cents\" — for advanced players and the curious

Double Bass Coach is new, and so far it has been tested on a small number of phones and basses. It might not hear your setup perfectly yet.\n\nIf a note isn\'t picked up or a score seems wrong, you can record a practice report and email it to the developer — that\'s exactly how the app gets better. You\'ll find it under Settings → Help improve the app.
Last thing — let the app hear your bass

Every phone microphone hears a double bass differently. The set-up listens to your room and a few notes you play, so your notes are scored correctly. It takes about two minutes, once.\n\nEverything you chose here can be changed later in Settings.
